_comment: Allowlist of license-cleared generator voice models. `generate` only uses models listed here unless --allow-unverified is passed. Each entry is diligence done ONCE, then enforced forever by the ship gate. Adding a model means you have read its license AND its training-data license and confirmed commercial + redistributable rights. NOT legal advice — good-faith reads of published model cards; confirm before a commercial release.

models.voice_traits: US English, female, single speaker, trained from scratch on public-domain data
models.notes: Clean transitive chain: trained FROM SCRATCH on the public-domain LJ Speech corpus (not fine-tuned from a restricted base). Default female base. Provenance traced to the primary dataset source (dataset_source_url), not just the model card. Re-confirm before commercial release.
models.dataset_provenance: Primary source: dataset author states the texts (published 1884-1964) and the 2016-17 LibriVox audio are public domain, with no restrictions on use. 13,100 single-speaker clips, ~24h, public-domain non-fiction. Verified against keithito.com/LJ-Speech-Dataset 2026-06. Good-faith read, not legal advice; gate enforces.

models.dataset: LibriVox public-domain recordings (~15.5h), compiled by Bryce Beattie
models.voice_traits: US English, male, single speaker, trained from scratch (1200 epochs, medium)
models.notes: Default MALE base. Same creator as the LJ Speech voice (Bryce Beattie); trained FROM SCRATCH on public-domain LibriVox recordings, dedicated public domain, creator grants use for 'any legal and ethical purpose.' CAVEAT: provenance here is the creator's own attestation on his personal site (not an institutional HuggingFace model card like LJ Speech) — credible and same trusted source, but a personal statement; re-confirm before commercial release. Download norman.onnx + .onnx.json from source_url. Alternatives from same source: 'John' (finetuned from Kristin — extra chain link), 'Bryce' (creator's own voice, ~750 samples).

models.dataset: LibriVox public-domain recordings (~24h), compiled by Bryce Beattie
models.voice_traits: UK English, female, single speaker, trained from scratch (500 epochs, high)
models.notes: Clean chain: trained FROM SCRATCH on public-domain LibriVox recordings, creator-dedicated public domain. Same trusted creator as LJ/Norman (Bryce Beattie). CAVEAT: creator's personal-site attestation, not an institutional model card — credible, same source, but re-confirm before commercial release. Good-faith read, NOT legal advice; gate enforces.

models.dataset: LibriVox public-domain recordings (~11.5h), compiled by Bryce Beattie
models.voice_traits: US English, female, single speaker, trained from scratch (2000 epochs, medium)
models.notes: Clean chain: trained FROM SCRATCH on public-domain LibriVox recordings, creator-dedicated public domain. Same creator as LJ/Norman. CAVEAT: creator's personal-site attestation, not institutional; re-confirm before commercial release. Good-faith read, NOT legal advice; gate enforces.

models.dataset: LibriVox public-domain recordings (~12.5h), compiled by Bryce Beattie
models.voice_traits: US English, male, single speaker; FINETUNED from 'Kristin' (not from scratch)
models.notes: Clean chain BUT note the extra link: FINETUNED from the Kristin voice (itself clean public-domain LibriVox), on ~12.5h public-domain LibriVox. Base + data both public domain, so chain is clean, but it's a fine-tune lineage rather than pure from-scratch — documented for diligence. Same creator (Bryce Beattie), personal-site attestation. Good-faith read, NOT legal advice; gate enforces.

_excluded_models.reason_excluded: Dataset (Jenny/Dioco) is ATTRIBUTION-REQUIRED, CC-BY-style — NOT public domain. Creator imposes no further restriction but cannot launder the underlying dataset's CC-BY obligation. Tonally appealing (Irish UK female) but excluded from the shippable/clean tier. Would require deliberate attribution-surfacing policy to use commercially.

Trained on LibriTTS-r, which is CC BY 4.0 — attribution required, NOT public domain. Excluded from the clean/shippable tier for the same reason as Jenny: creator's friendly grant does not override the dataset's CC-BY.

_notes_other_voices: ManyVoice (16-speaker, public-domain LibriVox, clean) NOT added pending multi-speaker addressing support. 'Bryce' (creator's own voice, public domain) clean license-wise but is a named individual's cloned voice — publicity-rights consideration separate from copyright; deferred.
